headline: How The Walt Disney (DIS) Investment Story Is Shifting As Analyst Views Rebalance
summary: Disney’s fair value estimate has been trimmed slightly from US$130.30 to US$129.23, a move that signals only a very small reset in price expectations. This shift sits alongside a mix of bullish and cautious analyst views, with some firms highlighting confidence in Disney’s intellectual property and execution, while others cut targets by US$3 to US$25 on concerns about current fundamentals and risk. As you read on, you will see how these competing views shape the evolving analyst narrative...

headline: AI animation studio Toonstar, HarperCollins team up on animated book projects
summary: Artificial intelligence powered animation studio Toonstar on Thursday unveiled a multi-year partnership with ‌News Corp-owned HarperCollins to produce a ‌slate of original animated series based on select titles ​from the book publisher.  The partnership aims to expand access to HarperCollins' literary catalog through digital-first animation.  The first project in development under ‌the deal is "Friendship ⁠List", based on Lisa Greenwald's popular series for tweens about friendship, self-discovery ⁠and the challenges of growing up.

headline: Is Disney’s Dividend Safe? A $50M Settlement and a New CEO Put It to the Test
summary: Walt Disney (NYSE: DIS) spans theme parks, cruise lines, streaming, and a film studio behind some of history’s highest-grossing franchises. The stock is down 15.1% year to date, trading around $96, which puts the $1.50 annual dividend at a yield of roughly 1.6%.$96, which puts the $1.50 annual dividend at a yield of roughly 1.6%. ... headline: 2026 Ascend A-List Awards: Honoring Business Leaders Shaping the Future
summary: The Ascend Foundation proudly hosts its annual A-List Awards, recognizing a distinguished group of global executives whose leadership is shaping industries, uplifting communities, and advancing opportunity at scale. More than a celebration, this annual benefit gala is the organization's most important night of the year—an exclusive gathering of influential leaders, partners, and supporters committed to building a more inclusive future.

headline: Netflix searches for franchises after losing out on Harry Potter
summary: After losing its bid for Warner Bros Discovery’s rich trove of characters and stories, Netflix is forging ahead with the challenging work of building culture-defining franchises on its own.  Chief Creative Officer Bela Bajaria said Netflix would keep investing in original ideas, and partner with established studios like MGM and Warner Bros, to try and produce movies and series that live on for years, in the vein ‌of “Stranger Things,” “Wednesday” and “Bridgerton.”  Yet the failed attempt to buy Warner Bros' storied movie studio and HBO highlighted a vulnerability for the relative Hollywood upstart, whose catalog of original films and ‌series spans around a dozen years, compared with more than a century's worth of stories and characters for Warner Bros, Walt Disney and Universal Pictures.

headline: Wells Fargo has a surprising take on Disney stock
summary: Disney (DIS) stock has quietly fallen out of favor, with shares down 14% so far this year and more than 50% from all-time highs. Streaming is now generating real profit, but that progress is being tested by weak near-term cash flow and fresh pressure in Sports as ESPN moves toward its ...
